title: "Wait" Should Not Be Invoked When Multiple Locks Are Held
description: Invoking 'wait' while holding multiple locks can lead to a deadlock, as 'wait' releases only one lock. The other lock remains held until another thread requests a lock on the awaited object, which could potentially lock out all other threads.
recommendation: Avoid calling 'wait' when multiple locks are held. Redesign the code to ensure that 'wait' is only invoked when a single lock is held.
impact: Prevents potential deadlocks that can cause the entire system to hang, affecting both code quality and system stability.

checks.message: Avoid calling 'wait' when multiple locks are held.
